summary: Iteration 4: Full backend + frontend verification of Telegram-integrated lead pipeline for AUTOHAUS. 11/11 pytest backend cases pass (POST /api/leads happy path with full configurator + UTM/device/referrer/page_url, list ordering desc, validation 400 for empty name / empty phone / non-digit phone / name>200, honeypot silent-200 with NO persistence, rate-limit 5/60s returns 429 on 6th, telegram_service.build_lead_message structure including HTML config block, analytics block, money formatting, omission of empty/false booleans, minimal payload variant). Frontend e2e via Playwright: Configurator selections (film=Hybrid, finish=Stealth, coverage=half) + name/phone submit -> SuccessOverlay with 'Заявка отправлена.', form cleared, UTM passthrough from URL (utm_source/campaign/medium/content/term) captured into POST body, honeypot input present in DOM but absolutely positioned at x=-9999 (invisible). ContactForm submit shows overlay, clears all fields (name/phone/car), UTM persisted from sessionStorage across forms. Success overlay auto-closes ~3.8s + exit anim (verified count=0 after 5.2s), manual close button works, ESC binding present, empty-form submit shows 'Заполните имя и телефон' toast. 0 console errors. Telegram chat ID 7505435012: not spammed - reasonable test count used.
critical_code_review_comments: telegram_service.send_lead_to_telegram swallows HTTP 400 from Telegram (e.g. chat hasn't started the bot) and only logs at ERROR level — this is correct per spec (telegram_sent=false is acceptable) but consider adding a /api/health/telegram endpoint or a one-time getMe sanity check at startup for ops.

Honeypot triggered branch in server.py returns Lead(name=payload.name or 'spam', phone=payload.phone or 'spam') — name/phone may exceed length or fail validation if a real bot fills them with very long junk; consider unconditionally returning a dummy Lead(name='spam', phone='spam') to avoid leaking any validation surface even on the silent path.
Rate limiter is in-memory (per-process) — works for single-replica supervisor setup but will not be shared across pods if scaled; document this limitation.

build_lead_message renders 'Чёрные элементы' (darkout) and similar booleans only when True — verified in tests; good UX. However, when ALL configuration values are False/None, the entire ⚙️ Конфигурация block is skipped, which is correct.

Both Configurator and ContactForm import their own <SuccessOverlay/> instance — both fire 3.8s timer + 0.7s exit anim independently; if a user submits both forms in succession the second overlay re-arms its timer correctly (verified).
Frontend axios POST does not include any client-side timeout; consider axios.create({ timeout: 15000 }) to surface backend hangs as 'Ошибка отправки' instead of indefinite spinner.
seed_data_creation: Created ~10 TEST_-prefixed leads through pytest (TEST_Cfg User, TEST_UTM_*, TEST_Sort0/1, TEST_RL0-4, TEST_HONEYPOT_* dropped) and 3 TEST_-prefixed leads via Playwright UI (TEST_PWUser, TEST_ContactU, TEST_AutoClose). All persisted in test_database.leads except honeypot (correctly dropped).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 13 spec items from features_or_bugs_to_test verified pass. Backend rate-limit is per X-Forwarded-For so tests synthesise distinct IPs; the live preview uses ingress so end-user IP rate limit may behave differently than tests but the 429 behavior is confirmed. Telegram bot @autohaus_leads_bot may or may not have started chat 7505435012 — telegram_sent=true was observed on at least one call (full_configurator_payload), confirming the bot CAN deliver. Honeypot field at #configurator + #contact has identical inline style position:absolute left:-9999px width:1px height:1px opacity:0. SuccessOverlay timer = 3.8s + framer-motion exit 0.7s; total DOM removal ~4.5-5.0s after open.
rca of the issue: n/a — no issues found this iteration.
